Code fragment:
public final TableField<BranchRecord, Long> ID =
    createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key is named just 'id' which is a generic name that doesn't convey any meaning about the table it belongs to. Consider renaming it to 'branch_id' to make it more descriptive and to avoid ambiguity in queries with joins.

public final TableField<LinkRecord, Long> ID =
    createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table uses a generic 'id' column as the primary key while a unique constraint on 'URL' already exists (CONSTRAINT_242), which could serve as a natural primary key. Consider using the URL as the primary key or renaming the synthetic key to something more descriptive like 'link_id'.

Code fragment:
public final TableField<TrackingRecord, Long> ID =
    createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table uses a generic 'id' column as primary key, but the natural composite key (chat_id, link_id) would be more appropriate since this is a junction table representing a many-to-many relationship between Chat and Link. Consider using a composite primary key on (chat_id, link_id) instead of a synthetic 'id' column.

Using selectFrom(LINK) fetches all columns from the LINK table, which may retrieve unnecessary data. Replace with an explicit column selection like dsl.select(LINK.ID, LINK.URL, LINK.CREATED_AT, LINK.CHECKED_AT, LINK.UPDATED_AT).from(LINK).

Using selectFrom(LINK) fetches all columns from the LINK table implicitly. Specify only the required columns explicitly, such as dsl.select(LINK.ID, LINK.URL, LINK.CREATED_AT, LINK.CHECKED_AT, LINK.UPDATED_AT).from(LINK).

Using `selectFrom(LINK)` fetches all columns from the LINK table into LinkRecord objects, even though only specific columns may be needed. Consider using `select()` with explicit column names like `dsl.select(LINK.ID, LINK.URL, LINK.CHECKED_AT, LINK.UPDATED_AT).from(LINK)` to fetch only the required columns.
Using `selectFrom(TRACKING)` fetches all columns from the TRACKING table, but only `linkId` is used. Replace with `dsl.select(TRACKING.LINK_ID).from(TRACKING)` to fetch only the required column.
